In [2]:
# ============================================================================
# REAL STOCK PRICE ANALYSIS: NVDA vs AAPL / MSFT / GOOG (2021 - TODAY)
# ============================================================================

import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import yfinance as yf

plt.style.use("seaborn-v0_8-darkgrid")
plt.rcParams["figure.figsize"] = (14, 8)

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# SETTINGS
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

tickers = ["NVDA", "AAPL", "MSFT", "GOOG"]
start_date = "2021-01-01"

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# DOWNLOAD REAL DATA  (auto_adjust=True => Close is already adjusted)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

print("Downloading real price data...")
data = yf.download(tickers, start=start_date, auto_adjust=True)
print("Download complete.")
print("Raw data shape:", data.shape)
print("Column index type:", type(data.columns))

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# GET CLOSE PRICES (WHICH ARE ADJUSTED)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

if isinstance(data.columns, pd.MultiIndex):
    # yfinance multi-ticker default: level 0 = OHLCV, level 1 = ticker
    prices = data["Close"].copy()        # pick the Close slice
    prices = prices[tickers]             # keep columns in desired order
else:
    # Flat columns case – keep only the Close columns, then clean their names
    close_cols = [c for c in data.columns if "Close" in str(c)]
    if len(close_cols) == 0:
        raise KeyError("No 'Close' columns found in downloaded data.")
    prices = data[close_cols].copy()

    # Try to recover ticker name before space or underscore
    new_names = []
    for c in prices.columns:
        ticker = str(c).split(" ")[0].split("_")[0]
        new_names.append(ticker)
    prices.columns = new_names
    prices = prices[[t for t in tickers if t in prices.columns]]

print("\nCleaned Close (adjusted) prices:")
print(prices.head())
print("Final prices shape:", prices.shape)

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# CUMULATIVE RETURNS
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

cumulative_returns = (prices / prices.iloc[0] - 1) * 100

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# ROLLING 1-YEAR RETURNS (252 TRADING DAYS)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

rolling_returns = (prices / prices.shift(252) - 1) * 100

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# PERFORMANCE STATS
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

daily_returns = prices.pct_change().dropna()

stats = pd.DataFrame({
    "Total Return %": cumulative_returns.iloc[-1],
    "Annualized Return %": daily_returns.mean() * 252 * 100,
    "Volatility %": daily_returns.std() * np.sqrt(252) * 100,
    "Sharpe Ratio": (daily_returns.mean() / daily_returns.std()) * np.sqrt(252)
}).round(3)

print("\n==================== PERFORMANCE STATS ====================")
print(stats)
print("============================================================\n")

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# PLOTS
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

# 1) PRICE LEVEL COMPARISON
plt.figure()
for t in prices.columns:
    plt.plot(prices.index, prices[t], label=t, linewidth=2)
plt.title("Stock Prices (Adj via auto_adjust) — NVDA vs Big Tech (2021–Present)")
plt.xlabel("Date")
plt.ylabel("Price ($)")
plt.legend()
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

# 2) CUMULATIVE RETURNS PLOT
plt.figure()
for t in prices.columns:
    plt.plot(cumulative_returns.index, cumulative_returns[t], label=t, linewidth=2)
plt.title("Cumulative Returns (%) — NVDA vs Big Tech")
plt.xlabel("Date")
plt.ylabel("Cumulative Return (%)")
plt.legend()
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

# 3) ROLLING RETURNS PLOT
plt.figure()
for t in prices.columns:
    plt.plot(rolling_returns.index, rolling_returns[t], label=t, linewidth=2)
plt.title("Rolling 1-Year Returns (%) — NVDA vs Big Tech")
plt.xlabel("Date")
plt.ylabel("1-Year Rolling Return (%)")
plt.legend()
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

Downloading real price data...

In [3]:
# ============================================================================
# AI STOCKS VS INDICES – ROLLING 252-DAY RETURNS (2021–PRESENT)
# NVDA, TSM, AMD, PLTR vs SPY, QQQ, ^DJI
# ============================================================================

import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import yfinance as yf

plt.style.use("seaborn-v0_8-darkgrid")
plt.rcParams["figure.figsize"] = (14, 8)

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# SETTINGS
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

ai_tickers = ["NVDA", "TSM", "AMD", "PLTR"]
index_tickers = ["^GSPC", "^IXIC", "^DJI"]
all_tickers = ai_tickers + index_tickers

start_date = "2020-01-01"      # need 1 year of history to get 2021 rolling windows
plot_start = "2021-01-01"      # only show rolling returns from 2021 onward

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# DOWNLOAD DATA (auto_adjust=True => Close is already adjusted)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

print("Downloading real price data...")
data = yf.download(all_tickers, start=start_date, auto_adjust=True)
print("Download complete.")
print("Raw data shape:", data.shape)
print("Column index type:", type(data.columns))

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# EXTRACT CLOSE PRICES
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def get_close_prices(df, tickers):
    cols = df.columns

    # Case 1: MultiIndex columns (field x ticker)
    if isinstance(cols, pd.MultiIndex):
        prices = df["Close"].copy()
        prices = prices[[t for t in tickers if t in prices.columns]]
        return prices

    # Case 2: Flat columns (e.g. 'NVDA_Close', 'SPY Close')
    close_cols = [c for c in cols if "Close" in str(c)]
    if len(close_cols) == 0:
        raise KeyError("No 'Close' columns found in downloaded data.")

    prices = df[close_cols].copy()

    new_names = []
    for c in prices.columns:
        ticker = str(c).split(" ")[0].split("_")[0]
        new_names.append(ticker)
    prices.columns = new_names

    prices = prices[[t for t in tickers if t in prices.columns]]
    return prices

prices = get_close_prices(data, all_tickers)

print("\nCleaned Close prices:")
print(prices.head())
print("Final prices shape:", prices.shape)

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# ROLLING 252-DAY RETURNS (%)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

rolling_252 = (prices / prices.shift(252) - 1) * 100

# Trim to 2021+ so the graph starts once full 1-year windows exist
rolling_252 = rolling_252.loc[rolling_252.index >= plot_start]

roll_ai = rolling_252[ai_tickers]
roll_idx = rolling_252[index_tickers]

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# BASIC STATS ON ROLLING RETURNS (OPTIONAL)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

stats_rolling = pd.DataFrame({
    "Avg 252d Return %": rolling_252.mean(),
    "Min 252d Return %": rolling_252.min(),
    "Max 252d Return %": rolling_252.max(),
}).round(2)

print("\n================ ROLLING 252-DAY RETURN STATS =================")
print(stats_rolling)
print("==============================================================\n")

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# PLOTS – ROLLING 1-YEAR RETURNS ONLY
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

# 1) AI STOCKS: Rolling 252-day returns
plt.figure()
for t in ai_tickers:
    plt.plot(roll_ai.index, roll_ai[t], label=t, linewidth=2)
plt.axhline(0, linewidth=1)
plt.title("AI Stocks – Rolling 252-Day Returns (%)")
plt.xlabel("Date")
plt.ylabel("Rolling 1-Year Return (%)")
plt.legend()
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

# 2) INDICES: Rolling 252-day returns
plt.figure()
for t in index_tickers:
    plt.plot(roll_idx.index, roll_idx[t], label=t, linewidth=2)
plt.axhline(0, linewidth=1)
plt.title("SPY / QQQ / ^DJI – Rolling 252-Day Returns (%)")
plt.xlabel("Date")
plt.ylabel("Rolling 1-Year Return (%)")
plt.legend()
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

# 3) AI vs INDICES on one chart (dashed for indices)
plt.figure()
for t in ai_tickers:
    plt.plot(roll_ai.index, roll_ai[t], label=t, linewidth=2)
for t in index_tickers:
    plt.plot(roll_idx.index, roll_idx[t],
             linestyle="--", linewidth=2, label=t + " (Index)")
plt.axhline(0, linewidth=1)
plt.title("Rolling 252-Day Returns (%) — AI Stocks vs SPY / QQQ / ^DJI")
plt.xlabel("Date")
plt.ylabel("Rolling 1-Year Return (%)")
plt.legend()
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

# ============================================================================
# AI AVERAGE VS OIL & GAS (XLE) — ROLLING 252-DAY RETURNS
# ============================================================================

import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import yfinance as yf

plt.style.use("seaborn-v0_8-darkgrid")
plt.rcParams["figure.figsize"] = (14, 8)

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# SETTINGS
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

ai_tickers = ["NVDA", "TSM", "AMD", "PLTR"]
oil_ticker = "XLE"

all_tickers = ai_tickers + [oil_ticker]

start_date = "2020-01-01"
plot_start = "2021-01-01"

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# DOWNLOAD DATA
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

print("Downloading data...")
data = yf.download(all_tickers, start=start_date, auto_adjust=True)
print("Download complete.")

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# CLEAN CLOSE PRICES
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def get_close_prices(df, tickers):
    cols = df.columns

    if isinstance(cols, pd.MultiIndex):
        prices = df["Close"].copy()
        prices = prices[[t for t in tickers if t in prices.columns]]
        return prices

    close_cols = [c for c in cols if "Close" in str(c)]
    prices = df[close_cols].copy()

    new_names = []
    for c in prices.columns:
        ticker = str(c).split(" ")[0].split("_")[0]
        new_names.append(ticker)
    prices.columns = new_names

    return prices[tickers]

prices = get_close_prices(data, all_tickers)

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# ROLLING 252-DAY RETURNS (%)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

rolling_252 = (prices / prices.shift(252) - 1) * 100
rolling_252 = rolling_252.loc[rolling_252.index >= plot_start]

# AI average
ai_avg = rolling_252[ai_tickers].mean(axis=1)

# Oil & Gas (XLE)
oil = rolling_252[oil_ticker]

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# PLOT — AI Average vs Oil/Gas
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

plt.figure()
plt.plot(ai_avg.index, ai_avg, "r-", linewidth=3, label="AI Average (NVDA/TSM/AMD/PLTR)")
plt.plot(oil.index, oil, "b-", linewidth=3, label="Oil & Gas Sector (XLE)")

plt.axhline(0, linewidth=1, color="gray")
plt.title("Rolling 252-Day Returns: AI Sector (Average) vs Oil & Gas (XLE)")
plt.xlabel("Date")
plt.ylabel("Rolling 1-Year Return (%)")
plt.legend()
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

Downloading data...

[*********************100%***********************]  5 of 5 completed

Download complete.

# ============================================================================
# AI AVERAGE VS OIL & GAS (XLE) — ROLLING 252-DAY ANNUALIZED VOLATILITY
# ============================================================================

import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import yfinance as yf

plt.style.use("seaborn-v0_8-darkgrid")
plt.rcParams["figure.figsize"] = (14, 8)

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# SETTINGS
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

ai_tickers = ["NVDA", "TSM", "AMD", "PLTR"]
oil_ticker = "XLE"

all_tickers = ai_tickers + [oil_ticker]

start_date = "2020-01-01"
plot_start = "2021-01-01"

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# DOWNLOAD DATA
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

print("Downloading data...")
data = yf.download(all_tickers, start=start_date, auto_adjust=True)
print("Download complete.")

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# CLEAN CLOSE PRICES
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def get_close_prices(df, tickers):
    cols = df.columns

    # MultiIndex case (field x ticker)
    if isinstance(cols, pd.MultiIndex):
        prices = df["Close"].copy()
        prices = prices[[t for t in tickers if t in prices.columns]]
        return prices

    # Flat columns case
    close_cols = [c for c in cols if "Close" in str(c)]
    prices = df[close_cols].copy()

    new_names = []
    for c in prices.columns:
        ticker = str(c).split(" ")[0].split("_")[0]
        new_names.append(ticker)
    prices.columns = new_names

    return prices[tickers]

prices = get_close_prices(data, all_tickers)

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# DAILY RETURNS
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

daily_returns = prices.pct_change()

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# ROLLING 252-DAY ANNUALIZED VOLATILITY (%)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

# rolling std of daily returns * sqrt(252) * 100 → annualized volatility in %
rolling_vol = daily_returns.rolling(window=252).std() * np.sqrt(252) * 100
rolling_vol = rolling_vol.loc[rolling_vol.index >= plot_start]

# AI average volatility
ai_vol_avg = rolling_vol[ai_tickers].mean(axis=1)

# Oil & Gas volatility
oil_vol = rolling_vol[oil_ticker]

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# PLOT — AI Average Volatility vs Oil/Gas Volatility
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

plt.figure()
plt.plot(ai_vol_avg.index, ai_vol_avg, "r--", linewidth=3,
         label="AI Average Volatility (NVDA/TSM/AMD/PLTR)")
plt.plot(oil_vol.index, oil_vol, "b-", linewidth=3,
         label="Oil & Gas Volatility (XLE)")

plt.title("Rolling 252-Day Annualized Volatility: AI Basket vs Oil & Gas (XLE)")
plt.xlabel("Date")
plt.ylabel("Annualized Volatility (%)")
plt.legend()
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

Downloading data...

[*********************100%***********************]  5 of 5 completed

Download complete.

# ============================================================================
# AI AVERAGE VOLATILITY VS NASDAQ (QQQ), S&P (SPY), DOW (DIA)
# Rolling 252-Day Annualized Volatility
# ============================================================================

import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import yfinance as yf

plt.style.use("seaborn-v0_8-darkgrid")
plt.rcParams["figure.figsize"] = (14, 8)

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# SETTINGS
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

ai_tickers = ["NVDA", "TSM", "AMD", "PLTR"]

index_tickers = {
    "NASDAQ (QQQ)": "QQQ",
    "S&P 500 (SPY)": "SPY",
    "Dow Jones (DIA)": "DIA"     # avoid ^DJI multiindex issues
}

all_tickers = ai_tickers + list(index_tickers.values())

start_date = "2020-01-01"
plot_start = "2021-01-01"

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# DOWNLOAD DATA
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

print("Downloading data...")
data = yf.download(all_tickers, start=start_date, auto_adjust=True)
print("Download complete.")

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# CLEAN CLOSE PRICES
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def get_close_prices(df, tickers):
    cols = df.columns

    # MultiIndex case
    if isinstance(cols, pd.MultiIndex):
        prices = df["Close"].copy()
        return prices[[t for t in tickers if t in prices.columns]]

    # Flat columns
    close_cols = [c for c in cols if "Close" in str(c)]
    prices = df[close_cols].copy()

    new_names = []
    for c in prices.columns:
        ticker = str(c).split(" ")[0].split("_")[0]
        new_names.append(ticker)
    prices.columns = new_names

    return prices[tickers]

prices = get_close_prices(data, all_tickers)

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# DAILY RETURNS
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

daily_returns = prices.pct_change()

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# ROLLING 252-DAY ANNUALIZED VOLATILITY (%)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

rolling_vol = daily_returns.rolling(window=252).std() * np.sqrt(252) * 100
rolling_vol = rolling_vol.loc[rolling_vol.index >= plot_start]

# AI average volatility
ai_vol_avg = rolling_vol[ai_tickers].mean(axis=1)

# Index volatilities
qqq_vol = rolling_vol[index_tickers["NASDAQ (QQQ)"]]
spy_vol = rolling_vol[index_tickers["S&P 500 (SPY)"]]
dia_vol = rolling_vol[index_tickers["Dow Jones (DIA)"]]

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# PLOT
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

plt.figure()

# AI average volatility — red dotted
plt.plot(ai_vol_avg.index, ai_vol_avg, "r--", linewidth=3,
         label="AI Average Volatility (NVDA/TSM/AMD/PLTR)")

# Indices
plt.plot(qqq_vol.index, qqq_vol, color="purple", linewidth=2,
         label="NASDAQ () Volatility")

plt.plot(spy_vol.index, spy_vol, color="green", linewidth=2,
         label="S&P 500 (GSCP) Volatility")

plt.plot(dia_vol.index, dia_vol, color="blue", linewidth=2,
         label="Dow Jones (DIA) Volatility")

plt.title("Rolling 252-Day Annualized Volatility — AI Basket vs Major Indices")
plt.xlabel("Date")
plt.ylabel("Annualized Volatility (%)")
plt.legend()
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

Downloading data...

Either fill in any non-leading NA values prior to calling pct_change or specify 'fill_method=None' to not fill NA values.

# ============================================================================
# AI STOCKS VS BIG TECH — ROLLING 252-DAY RETURNS (2021–PRESENT)
# Thin solid lines only
# ============================================================================

import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import yfinance as yf

plt.style.use("seaborn-v0_8-darkgrid")
plt.rcParams["figure.figsize"] = (14, 8)

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# SETTINGS
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

ai_tickers = ["NVDA", "TSM", "AMD", "PLTR"]
bigtech_tickers = ["MSFT", "GOOG", "AAPL", "META"]

all_tickers = ai_tickers + bigtech_tickers

start_date = "2020-01-01"
plot_start = "2021-01-01"

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# DOWNLOAD DATA
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

print("Downloading real price data...")
data = yf.download(all_tickers, start=start_date, auto_adjust=True)
print("Download complete.")

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# CLEAN CLOSE PRICES
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def get_close_prices(df, tickers):
    cols = df.columns

    # MultiIndex case
    if isinstance(cols, pd.MultiIndex):
        prices = df["Close"].copy()
        return prices[[t for t in tickers if t in prices.columns]]

    # Flat columns
    close_cols = [c for c in cols if "Close" in str(c)]
    prices = df[close_cols].copy()

    new_names = []
    for c in prices.columns:
        ticker = str(c).split(" ")[0].split("_")[0]
        new_names.append(ticker)
    prices.columns = new_names

    return prices[tickers]

prices = get_close_prices(data, all_tickers)

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# ROLLING 252-DAY RETURNS (%)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

rolling_252 = (prices / prices.shift(252) - 1) * 100
rolling_252 = rolling_252.loc[rolling_252.index >= plot_start]

roll_ai = rolling_252[ai_tickers]
roll_bigtech = rolling_252[bigtech_tickers]

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# PLOT — ALL LINES SOLID + THIN
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

plt.figure()

for t in ai_tickers:
    plt.plot(roll_ai.index, roll_ai[t], linewidth=1, label=f"{t}")

for t in bigtech_tickers:
    plt.plot(roll_bigtech.index, roll_bigtech[t], linewidth=1, label=f"{t}")

plt.axhline(0, linewidth=0.8, color="gray")
plt.title("Rolling 252-Day Returns (%) — AI Stocks vs Big Tech")
plt.xlabel("Date")
plt.ylabel("Rolling 1-Year Return (%)")
plt.legend()
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

Downloading real price data...

[*********************100%***********************]  8 of 8 completed

In [20]:
# ============================================================================
# AI STOCKS (AMD, TSM, NVDA, PLTR) VS S&P / NASDAQ / DOW
# CUMULATIVE PERCENT RETURNS (NORMALIZED FROM EACH TICKER'S FIRST PRICE)
# ============================================================================

import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import yfinance as yf

plt.style.use("seaborn-v0_8-darkgrid")
plt.rcParams["figure.figsize"] = (14, 8)

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# SETTINGS
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

ai_tickers = ["AMD", "TSM", "NVDA", "PLTR"]
index_tickers = ["SPY", "QQQ", "DIA"]   # S&P 500, Nasdaq 100, Dow via ETFs
all_tickers = ai_tickers + index_tickers

start_date = "2021-01-01"

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# DOWNLOAD DATA
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

print("Downloading real price data...")
data = yf.download(all_tickers, start=start_date, auto_adjust=True)
print("Download complete.")
print("Raw data shape:", data.shape)
print("Column index type:", type(data.columns))

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# EXTRACT CLOSE PRICES ROBUSTLY
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def get_close_prices(df, tickers):
    cols = df.columns

    # Case 1: MultiIndex (field x ticker)
    if isinstance(cols, pd.MultiIndex):
        prices = df["Close"].copy()
        prices = prices[[t for t in tickers if t in prices.columns]]
        return prices

    # Case 2: Flat columns (e.g. 'AMD_Close', 'SPY Close', etc.)
    close_cols = [c for c in cols if "Close" in str(c)]
    if len(close_cols) == 0:
        raise KeyError("No 'Close' columns found in downloaded data.")

    prices = df[close_cols].copy()

    # Clean column names back to ticker symbols
    new_names = []
    for c in prices.columns:
        ticker = str(c).split(" ")[0].split("_")[0]
        new_names.append(ticker)
    prices.columns = new_names

    prices = prices[[t for t in tickers if t in prices.columns]]
    return prices

prices = get_close_prices(data, all_tickers)

print("\nCleaned Close prices:")
print(prices.head())
print("Final prices shape:", prices.shape)

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# CUMULATIVE PERCENT RETURNS
# Normalize EACH ticker from its own first valid price
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

cumulative_returns = pd.DataFrame(index=prices.index, columns=prices.columns, dtype=float)

for col in prices.columns:
    first_idx = prices[col].first_valid_index()
    base_price = prices[col].loc[first_idx]
    cumulative_returns[col] = (prices[col] / base_price - 1) * 100

# Optional: drop rows where ALL are NaN (before any of them started trading)
cumulative_returns = cumulative_returns.dropna(how="all")

# Final % return as of latest date
final_returns = cumulative_returns.iloc[-1].round(2)

print("\n============= TOTAL PERCENT RETURN (FROM EACH TICKER'S FIRST PRICE) =============")
print(final_returns.rename("Total Return %"))
print("==============================================================================\n")

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# PLOT CUMULATIVE % RETURNS
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

plt.figure()

for t in all_tickers:
    plt.plot(cumulative_returns.index,
             cumulative_returns[t],
             linewidth=1,
             label=t)

plt.axhline(0, linewidth=0.8, color="gray")
plt.title("Cumulative Returns (%) — AI Stocks vs S&P / Nasdaq / Dow")
plt.xlabel("Date")
plt.ylabel("Cumulative Return (%)")
plt.legend()
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

#####-------------------------------------------------############
#####---------MONTE CARLO SIMULATION PALANTIR----------############
#####-------------------------------------------------##############

import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 1. BASIC INPUTS (ADJUST THESE AS YOU LIKE)
# ------------------------------------------------------------

# ---- Fundamental starting point (you can update with latest data) ----
# Approximate starting values – feel free to plug in your own.
# ---- Palantir DCF Starting Point (Q3 2025 Actuals) ----

starting_revenue = 4_400_000_000        # FY2025 revenue guidance (~$4.396–4.40B)
starting_fcf_margin = 0.45              # Current FCF margin ~45–46% (Q3 FCF margin = 46%)
net_cash = 6_400_000_000                # Cash + equivalents + USTs; zero debt
shares_outstanding = 2_220_000_000      # ~2.21–2.23B diluted; 2.22B is fair
forecast_years = 10


# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 2. SCENARIO GRIDS (DETERMINISTIC, NOT RANDOM)
#    10 values each -> 10,000 total combinations
# ------------------------------------------------------------
revenue_cagr_grid = np.linspace(0.10, 0.30, 10)     # 10% to 30%
terminal_margin_grid = np.linspace(0.18, 0.35, 10)  # 18% to 35%
wacc_grid = np.linspace(0.08, 0.12, 10)             # 8% to 12%
terminal_growth_grid = np.linspace(0.015, 0.05, 10)  # 1.5% to 5%

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 3. OPTIONAL: GET CURRENT MARKET PRICE FROM YFINANCE
# ------------------------------------------------------------
try:
    import yfinance as yf
    pltr = yf.Ticker("PLTR")
    # Use 'Close' to avoid Adj Close errors
    current_price = pltr.history(period="1d")["Close"].iloc[-1]
except Exception as e:
    print("Could not fetch current PLTR price from yfinance. "
          "Set manually if you want comparison.")
    print("Error:", e)
    current_price = None

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 4. DCF FUNCTION
# ------------------------------------------------------------

def dcf_value_per_share(
    revenue_0,
    fcf_margin_0,
    fcf_margin_terminal,
    revenue_cagr,
    wacc,
    terminal_growth,
    years,
    net_cash,
    shares_out
):
    """
    Simple DCF:
      - Revenue grows at constant CAGR.  
      - FCF margin linearly ramps from fcf_margin_0 to fcf_margin_terminal.
      - Discount rate = wacc.
      - Terminal value via Gordon growth (perpetuity) using terminal_growth.
    """
    # Build arrays for each year
    t = np.arange(1, years + 1)

    # Revenue path
    revenues = revenue_0 * (1 + revenue_cagr) ** t

    # Margin path: linear ramp from current margin to terminal margin
    margins = np.linspace(fcf_margin_0, fcf_margin_terminal, years)

    # Free cash flow each year
    fcfs = revenues * margins

    # Discount factors
    discount_factors = (1 + wacc) ** t

    # Present value of yearly FCFs
    pv_fcfs = fcfs / discount_factors

    # Terminal value at year N (based on FCF in year N+1)
    fcf_N_plus_1 = fcfs[-1] * (1 + terminal_growth)
    # Avoid division by zero
    if wacc <= terminal_growth:
        # If this happens, the assumption set is unrealistic; return NaN
        return np.nan

    terminal_value = fcf_N_plus_1 / (wacc - terminal_growth)
    pv_terminal_value = terminal_value / ((1 + wacc) ** years)

    enterprise_value = pv_fcfs.sum() + pv_terminal_value
    equity_value = enterprise_value + net_cash

    value_per_share = equity_value / shares_out
    return value_per_share

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 5. BUILD THE 10,000-SCENARIO GRID AND RUN DCF
# ------------------------------------------------------------

results = []

for rev_cagr in revenue_cagr_grid:
    for term_margin in terminal_margin_grid:
        for wacc in wacc_grid:
            for g in terminal_growth_grid:
                val_ps = dcf_value_per_share(  
                    revenue_0=starting_revenue,
                    fcf_margin_0=starting_fcf_margin,  
                    fcf_margin_terminal=term_margin,
                    revenue_cagr=rev_cagr,
                    wacc=wacc,
                    terminal_growth=g,
                    years=forecast_years,
                    net_cash=net_cash,
                    shares_out=shares_outstanding
                )
                results.append(
                    {
                        "revenue_cagr": rev_cagr,
                        "terminal_margin": term_margin,
                        "wacc": wacc,
                        "terminal_growth": g,
                        "value_per_share": val_ps
                    }
                )

df = pd.DataFrame(results)
df = df.dropna()  # in case of any invalid combos

print(f"Total scenarios computed: {len(df)}")

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 6. BASIC STATS & TEXT OUTPUT
# ------------------------------------------------------------

descriptive = df["value_per_share"].describe(percentiles=[0.05, 0.25, 0.5, 0.75, 0.95])
print("\nDCF Value per Share - Scenario Statistics (USD):")
print(descriptive)

if current_price is not None:
    print(f"\nCurrent PLTR market price: ${current_price:,.2f}")
    median_val = descriptive["50%"]
    if median_val > current_price * 1.1:
        verdict = "undervalued in this scenario set (median > current price)"
    elif median_val < current_price * 0.9:
        verdict = "overvalued in this scenario set (median < current price)"
    else:
        verdict = "roughly fairly valued in this scenario set"
    print(f"Relative to these assumptions, PLTR appears {verdict}.")
else:
    print("\nCurrent price not available (yfinance issue). "
          "Install yfinance or set 'current_price' manually at the top.")

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 7. VISUALIZATIONS
# ------------------------------------------------------------

plt.style.use("default")

# ---- Histogram of intrinsic values ----
plt.figure(figsize=(10, 6))
plt.hist(df["value_per_share"], bins=50, edgecolor="black", alpha=0.7)
plt.xlabel("Intrinsic Value per Share (USD)")
plt.ylabel("Number of Scenarios")
plt.title("Palantir (PLTR) DCF - 10,000 Scenario Valuation Distribution")

# Plot median line
median_val = df["value_per_share"].median()
plt.axvline(median_val, linestyle="--", linewidth=2, label=f"Median: ${median_val:,.2f}")

# Plot current price if available
if current_price is not None:
    plt.axvline(current_price, linestyle="-", linewidth=2, label=f"Current Price: ${current_price:,.2f}")

plt.legend()
plt.tight_layout()

# ---- Heatmap: Value per share as function of revenue CAGR & terminal margin ----
# Filter scenarios around the median WACC and terminal growth for a cleaner 10x10 heatmap.
target_wacc = wacc_grid[len(wacc_grid) // 2]
target_g = terminal_growth_grid[len(terminal_growth_grid) // 2]

slice_df = df[
    (df["wacc"] == target_wacc) &
    (df["terminal_growth"] == target_g)
].copy()

# Pivot into 2D grid: rows = terminal margin, cols = revenue CAGR
heatmap_data = slice_df.pivot(
    index="terminal_margin",
    columns="revenue_cagr",
    values="value_per_share"
).sort_index(ascending=True)

plt.figure(figsize=(10, 6))
im = plt.imshow(heatmap_data, aspect="auto", origin="lower")

plt.colorbar(im, label="Value per Share (USD)")
plt.xticks(
    ticks=np.arange(len(heatmap_data.columns)),
    labels=[f"{x*100:.1f}%" for x in heatmap_data.columns],
    rotation=45
)
plt.yticks(
    ticks=np.arange(len(heatmap_data.index)),
    labels=[f"{y*100:.1f}%" for y in heatmap_data.index]
)

plt.xlabel("Revenue CAGR")
plt.ylabel("Terminal FCF Margin")
plt.title(
    f"PLTR DCF Heatmap (WACC={target_wacc*100:.1f}%, "
    f"g={target_g*100:.1f}%)"
)

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

Total scenarios computed: 10000

DCF Value per Share - Scenario Statistics (USD):

5%          16.198451
25%         23.371080

75%         48.827361
95%         79.868954

Relative to these assumptions, PLTR appears overvalued in this scenario set (median < current price).

import yfinance as yf
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import matplotlib.dates as mdates

# ==============================
# 1. Tickers
# ==============================
tickers = [
    "NVDA",
    "PLTR",
    "AMD",
    "TSM",
    "^DJI",
    "^GSPC",
    "^IXIC"
]

# Download prices starting in 2020
data = yf.download(
    tickers,
    start="2020-01-01",
    progress=False
)

# ==============================
# 2. Use ONLY the Close prices
# ==============================
close = data["Close"].copy()

# ==============================
# 3. Slice data to YTD starting 2021
# ==============================
ytd_start = "2021-01-01"
ytd_prices = close.loc[ytd_start:]

# Ensure all have first-day data
ytd_prices = ytd_prices.dropna(axis=1, how="any", subset=[ytd_prices.index[0]])

# ==============================
# 4. Compute YTD Returns (%)
# ==============================
ytd_returns = (ytd_prices / ytd_prices.iloc[0] - 1) * 100

# ==============================
# 5. Plot
# ==============================
plt.figure(figsize=(14, 7))

for ticker in ytd_returns.columns:
    plt.plot(ytd_returns.index, ytd_returns[ticker], label=ticker)

plt.title("YTD Returns Since Jan 1, 2021\n(NVDA, PLTR, AMD, TSM vs Dow, S&P 500, Nasdaq)")
plt.xlabel("Date")
plt.ylabel("Return (%)")
plt.grid(True, linestyle="--", alpha=0.4)

# Year markers on x-axis
ax = plt.gca()
ax.xaxis.set_major_locator(mdates.YearLocator())
ax.xaxis.set_major_formatter(mdates.DateFormatter('%Y'))
plt.xticks(rotation=45)

plt.legend(loc="upper left")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

In [11]:
"""
Monte Carlo DCF Valuation for Palantir (PLTR)
10,000 simulations with realistic financial bounds

Requirements:
    pip install numpy pandas matplotlib yfinance
"""

import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt


# ---------------------------------------------------------
# Fetch current PLTR price (optional)
# ---------------------------------------------------------
try:
    import yfinance as yf
    pltr = yf.Ticker("PLTR")
    current_price = pltr.history(period="1d")["Close"].iloc[-1]
except:
    current_price = None


# ---------------------------------------------------------
# Base inputs
# ---------------------------------------------------------
starting_revenue = 2.6e9   # TTM revenue
starting_fcf_margin = 0.15 # current-ish
net_cash = 3e9
shares_outstanding = 2.2e9
forecast_years = 10

N = 10000  # number of simulations


# ---------------------------------------------------------
# Random variable generators (clipped)
# ---------------------------------------------------------
def clipped_normal(mean, sd, low, high, size):
    vals = np.random.normal(mean, sd, size)
    return np.clip(vals, low, high)


# Revenue CAGR: 8–25%
rev_cagr = clipped_normal(0.15, 0.03, 0.08, 0.25, N)

# Terminal FCF Margin: 18–35%
term_margin = clipped_normal(0.25, 0.04, 0.18, 0.35, N)

# WACC: 8–12%
wacc = clipped_normal(0.095, 0.01, 0.08, 0.12, N)

# Terminal growth: 1–3.5%
g = clipped_normal(0.025, 0.005, 0.01, 0.035, N)


# ---------------------------------------------------------
# DCF valuation engine
# ---------------------------------------------------------
def run_dcf(revenue0, cagr, margin_terminal, wacc, g, years=10):
    t = np.arange(1, years + 1)

    # Revenue path
    revenues = revenue0 * (1 + cagr) ** t

    # Margin starts at current level and ramps to terminal
    margin_path = np.linspace(starting_fcf_margin, margin_terminal, years)

    # Free cash flow
    fcfs = revenues * margin_path

    # Discounted FCF
    pv_fcfs = fcfs / ((1 + wacc) ** t)

    # Terminal value
    fcf_N_plus_1 = fcfs[-1] * (1 + g)
    TV = fcf_N_plus_1 / (wacc - g)
    PV_TV = TV / ((1 + wacc) ** years)

    # Enterprise → Equity
    equity_value = pv_fcfs.sum() + PV_TV + net_cash
    intrinsic_value = equity_value / shares_outstanding

    return intrinsic_value


# ---------------------------------------------------------
# Run Monte Carlo Simulation
# ---------------------------------------------------------
values = np.zeros(N)

for i in np.arange(N):
    values[i] = run_dcf(
        revenue0=starting_revenue,
        cagr=rev_cagr[i],
        margin_terminal=term_margin[i],
        wacc=wacc[i],
        g=g[i],
        years=forecast_years
    )

df = pd.DataFrame({"value_per_share": values})


# ---------------------------------------------------------
# Summary Statistics
# ---------------------------------------------------------
print("\nPALANTIR MONTE CARLO DCF (10,000 SIMULATIONS)")
print("=============================================\n")
print(df["value_per_share"].describe())

if current_price:
    print(f"\nCurrent PLTR Price: ${current_price:.2f}")
    median_val = df["value_per_share"].median()
    if median_val > current_price * 1.1:
        verdict = "undervalued in this simulation."
    elif median_val < current_price * 0.9:
        verdict = "overvalued in this simulation."
    else:
        verdict = "near fair value in this simulation."
    print(f"Conclusion: PLTR appears {verdict}\n")


# ---------------------------------------------------------
# Visualization
# ---------------------------------------------------------
plt.figure(figsize=(10, 6))
plt.hist(values, bins=60, edgecolor='black', alpha=0.7)
plt.title("PLTR Monte Carlo DCF Valuation - Distribution of Outcomes")
plt.xlabel("Value per Share (USD)")
plt.ylabel("Number of Simulations")
plt.axvline(df.value_per_share.median(), color='red', linestyle='--', label=f"Median = ${df.value_per_share.median():.2f}")

if current_price:
    plt.axvline(current_price, color='green', linestyle='-', label=f"Current Price = ${current_price:.2f}")

plt.legend()
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

PALANTIR MONTE CARLO DCF (10,000 SIMULATIONS)
=============================================

count    10000.000000
mean        12.144798
std          3.523489
min          4.440024
25%          9.614191
50%         11.599105
75%         14.074194
max         34.100407
Name: value_per_share, dtype: float64

Current PLTR Price: $181.86
Conclusion: PLTR appears overvalued in this simulation.
In [12]:
"""
Single-Iteration DCF Valuation for Palantir (PLTR)

Assumptions based on recent data:
- Revenue (TTM): ~3.9B USD
- Net cash: ~6.2B USD
- Shares outstanding: ~2.383B
- FCF margin ramps from 18% to 25%
- Revenue CAGR 10y: 15%
- WACC: 10%
- Terminal growth: 2.5%

Requires: numpy, yfinance (optional, for current price)
"""

import numpy as np

# -----------------------------
# 1. Hard-coded "real" inputs
# -----------------------------
revenue_ttm = 3.9e9         # USD, trailing twelve months
net_cash = 6.2e9            # cash - debt
shares_outstanding = 2.383e9

# DCF assumptions (tweak these as you like)
forecast_years = 10
revenue_cagr = 0.15         # 15% annual growth for 10 years
starting_fcf_margin = 0.18  # 18% today
terminal_fcf_margin = 0.25  # 25% by year 10
wacc = 0.10                 # 10% discount rate
terminal_growth = 0.025     # 2.5% perpetual growth


# -----------------------------
# 2. DCF function
# -----------------------------
def dcf_value_per_share(
    revenue_0,
    revenue_cagr,
    margin_start,
    margin_terminal,
    wacc,
    g,
    years,
    net_cash,
    shares_out
):
    """
    Simple 10-year DCF:
      - Revenue grows at constant CAGR
      - FCF margin linearly ramps from margin_start to margin_terminal
      - Discount rate = wacc
      - Terminal value uses Gordon growth with rate g
    """

    t = np.arange(1, years + 1)

    # Revenue path
    revenues = revenue_0 * (1 + revenue_cagr) ** t

    # Margin path: straight-line ramp
    margins = np.linspace(margin_start, margin_terminal, years)

    # Free cash flow each year
    fcfs = revenues * margins

    # Discount yearly FCFs
    discount_factors = (1 + wacc) ** t
    pv_fcfs = fcfs / discount_factors

    # Terminal value at year N based on FCF in year N+1
    fcf_N_plus_1 = fcfs[-1] * (1 + g)

    if wacc <= g:
        raise ValueError("WACC must be greater than terminal growth rate g.")

    terminal_value = fcf_N_plus_1 / (wacc - g)
    pv_terminal_value = terminal_value / ((1 + wacc) ** years)

    # Enterprise value = PV of forecast period + PV of terminal value
    enterprise_value = pv_fcfs.sum() + pv_terminal_value

    # Equity value = EV + net cash
    equity_value = enterprise_value + net_cash

    # Per-share intrinsic value
    value_per_share = equity_value / shares_out
    return value_per_share, enterprise_value, equity_value, fcfs


# -----------------------------
# 3. Run the DCF
# -----------------------------
value_ps, ev, eq, fcfs = dcf_value_per_share(
    revenue_0=revenue_ttm,
    revenue_cagr=revenue_cagr,
    margin_start=starting_fcf_margin,
    margin_terminal=terminal_fcf_margin,
    wacc=wacc,
    g=terminal_growth,
    years=forecast_years,
    net_cash=net_cash,
    shares_out=shares_outstanding
)

print("====== Palantir (PLTR) - Single DCF Iteration ======")
print(f"Revenue (TTM):         ${revenue_ttm/1e9:,.2f} B")
print(f"Net cash:              ${net_cash/1e9:,.2f} B")
print(f"Shares outstanding:     {shares_outstanding/1e9:,.3f} B")
print()
print(f"Assumed revenue CAGR:   {revenue_cagr*100:.1f}%")
print(f"FCF margin (start → end): {starting_fcf_margin*100:.1f}% → {terminal_fcf_margin*100:.1f}%")
print(f"WACC (discount rate):   {wacc*100:.1f}%")
print(f"Terminal growth (g):    {terminal_growth*100:.1f}%")
print(f"Horizon:                {forecast_years} years")
print()
print(f"Enterprise value (EV):  ${ev/1e9:,.2f} B")
print(f"Equity value:           ${eq/1e9:,.2f} B")
print(f"Intrinsic value/share:  ${value_ps:,.2f}")

# -----------------------------
# 4. Optional: compare to live market price via yfinance
# -----------------------------
try:
    import yfinance as yf
    pltr = yf.Ticker("PLTR")
    current_price = pltr.history(period="1d")["Close"].iloc[-1]
    print()
    print(f"Current market price:   ${current_price:,.2f}")
    diff = current_price - value_ps
    premium_pct = diff / value_ps * 100
    print(f"Market premium vs DCF:  ${diff:,.2f} ({premium_pct:.1f}%)")
except Exception as e:
    print("\nCould not fetch current PLTR price (yfinance issue).")
    print("Error:", e)

====== Palantir (PLTR) - Single DCF Iteration ======
Revenue (TTM):         $3.90 B
Net cash:              $6.20 B
Shares outstanding:     2.383 B

Assumed revenue CAGR:   15.0%
FCF margin (start → end): 18.0% → 25.0%
WACC (discount rate):   10.0%
Terminal growth (g):    2.5%
Horizon:                10 years

Enterprise value (EV):  $31.72 B
Equity value:           $37.92 B
Intrinsic value/share:  $15.91

Current market price:   $181.86
Market premium vs DCF:  $165.95 (1042.8%)

######--------------------------####
######-----Bar Chart Code------######
######-------------------------#####
import yfinance as yf
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

tickers = ["NVDA", "AMD", "SPY", "TSM", "PLTR"]
start_date = "2020-01-01"
end_date = "2025-09-30"

# Download prices w auto-adjust
data = yf.download(
    tickers,
    start=start_date,
    end=end_date,
    auto_adjust=True,
    progress=False
)

# Close prices auto-adj
if isinstance(data.columns, pd.MultiIndex):
    prices = data["Close"]
else:
    prices = data

# Drop NaNs, returns, std dev
prices = prices.dropna(axis=1, how="all")
returns = prices.pct_change().dropna()
std_devs = returns.std()

# SPY moved to the left and chart creation
ordered_tickers = ["SPY"] + [t for t in std_devs.index if t != "SPY"]
std_devs = std_devs.loc[ordered_tickers]

plt.figure(figsize=(10, 6))
plt.bar(std_devs.index, std_devs.values)
plt.xlabel("Ticker")
plt.ylabel("Standard Deviation of Daily Returns")
plt.title("Standard Deviation of Returns (2020-01-01 to 2025-09-30)")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
In [14]:
import yfinance as yf
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns
import statsmodels.api as sm

plt.style.use("seaborn-v0_8")

# =============================
# Setup and data
# =============================

TICKERS = ["NVDA", "TSM", "AMD", "PLTR"]
MARKET = "SPY"
ALL = TICKERS + [MARKET]
ROLL = 60  # rolling window in days

data = yf.download(
    ALL,
    period="5y",
    interval="1d",
    auto_adjust=True,
    progress=False
)["Close"]

data = data.dropna()
returns = data.pct_change().dropna()

# =============================
# Model 1: Full correlation matrix
# =============================

corr = returns.corr()
display(corr)

plt.figure(figsize=(8, 6))
sns.heatmap(corr, annot=True, cmap="vlag", center=0, fmt=".2f")
plt.title("Model 1: 5 Year Correlation Matrix (Daily Returns)")
plt.show()

# =============================
# Model 2: Rolling correlation vs market
# =============================

rolling_corr = pd.DataFrame()

for t in TICKERS:
    rolling_corr[t] = returns[t].rolling(ROLL).corr(returns[MARKET])

plt.figure(figsize=(12, 6))
for t in TICKERS:
    plt.plot(rolling_corr.index, rolling_corr[t], label=t)

plt.title(f"Model 2: {ROLL} Day Rolling Correlation vs {MARKET}")
plt.axhline(0, color="black", linewidth=0.5)
plt.ylim(-1, 1)
plt.legend()
plt.show()

# =============================
# Model 3: Rolling beta vs market
# =============================

def compute_rolling_beta(df_returns, market, window):
    betas = pd.DataFrame(index=df_returns.index)
    market_var = df_returns[market].rolling(window).var()

    for stock in TICKERS:
        cov = df_returns[stock].rolling(window).cov(df_returns[market])
        betas[stock] = cov / market_var

    return betas

rolling_beta = compute_rolling_beta(returns, MARKET, ROLL)

plt.figure(figsize=(12, 6))
for t in TICKERS:
    plt.plot(rolling_beta.index, rolling_beta[t], label=t)

plt.title(f"Model 3: {ROLL} Day Rolling Beta vs {MARKET}")
plt.axhline(1, linestyle="--", color="black", linewidth=0.7)
plt.legend()
plt.show()

# =============================
# Model 4: Idiosyncratic volatility and R squared
# =============================

idio_vol = {}
r2_vals = {}

for t in TICKERS:
    y = returns[t]
    X = sm.add_constant(returns[MARKET])
    model = sm.OLS(y, X).fit()
    idio_vol[t] = model.resid.std()
    r2_vals[t] = model.rsquared

idio_df = pd.DataFrame({
    "Idiosyncratic Volatility (Std Residuals)": idio_vol,
    "R^2 vs Market": r2_vals
})

display(idio_df)

# =============================
# Model 5: Cumulative returns
# =============================

cum = (1 + returns).cumprod()

plt.figure(figsize=(12, 6))
for t in TICKERS:
    plt.plot(cum.index, cum[t], label=t)

plt.plot(cum.index, cum[MARKET], label=MARKET, linewidth=2, color="black")
plt.title("Model 5: Cumulative Returns Over 5 Years")
plt.legend()
plt.show()

# =============================
# Model 6: Cross sectional dispersion (AI basket)
# =============================

dispersion = returns[TICKERS].std(axis=1).rolling(ROLL).mean()

plt.figure(figsize=(12, 5))
plt.plot(dispersion.index, dispersion, label="AI Basket Dispersion")
plt.title(f"Model 6: {ROLL} Day Rolling Cross Sectional Dispersion")
plt.ylabel("Cross sectional std dev")
plt.legend()
plt.show()

import yfinance as yf
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import pandas as pd

# =============================
# Model 7: Normalized price paths since 2018
# =============================

tickers = ["NVDA", "AMD", "TSM", "PLTR", "SPY", "QQQ"]
data = yf.download(tickers, start="2018-01-01")["Close"]

normalized = data.apply(lambda x: x / x.dropna().iloc[0])

plt.figure(figsize=(12, 6))
normalized.plot()
plt.title("Model 7: Normalized Price Performance (Index = 1 at First Trading Day)")
plt.xlabel("Date")
plt.ylabel("Growth factor")
plt.legend()
plt.show()
